learning_objectives: Understand the game marketing landscape and its evolution
Identify the key phases of a game marketing campaign
Analyze successful and failed game launches from a marketing perspective
readings: Gamasutra: Anatomy of a Game Launch

lecture_notes.introduction: Game marketing has evolved from magazine ads and boxed retail to a complex digital ecosystem. This course prepares you to navigate that ecosystem and bring a game to market effectively.
key_concepts: The four pillars of game marketing: awareness, wishlists, conversion, retention
Marketing timeline from pre-production to post-launch
Target audience identification and persona development
Platform-specific marketing considerations (Steam, consoles, mobile)
The relationship between game quality and marketing effectiveness
lecture_notes.demonstration: Case study breakdown of three game launches: one AAA, one indie success, one indie failure
setup_requirements: Access to SteamSpy and Steam Charts for market data
Social media monitoring tools (Hootsuite or Buffer trial accounts)

review.value: Awareness leads to interest, interest leads to wishlists, wishlists convert to sales at roughly 15-20% on Steam
Ideally begins 12-18 months before launch with reveal, builds through development, peaks at launch

class_schedule.topic: Building a Press Kit and Media Assets
learning_objectives: Create a professional press kit using presskit.html or equivalent tools
Capture high-quality screenshots and key art
Write effective game descriptions for store pages and press outreach
readings: How to Write a Game Description That Sells - GDC Vault

lecture_notes.introduction: Your press kit is the single most important marketing asset you will create. Journalists, streamers, and curators all rely on it to decide whether your game is worth covering.
key_concepts: Essential press kit components: screenshots, logos, key art, factsheet, description, trailer, team bios
Screenshot composition rules: show gameplay, not menus; capture moments of action or beauty
Key art design principles for store visibility at thumbnail size
Writing game descriptions: hook sentence, core loop, unique selling points, comparison hooks
Maintaining a living press kit that evolves with development
lecture_notes.demonstration: Live creation of a press kit using presskit.html, populating it with sample game assets
setup_requirements: Image editing software (Photoshop, GIMP, or Figma)
Sample game project with screenshots available

review.value: Factsheet, description, 5-8 high-resolution screenshots, logo pack, key art, trailer embed, contact information
Hook sentence + genre/comparison + 3 unique features + call to action (wishlist/follow)

class_schedule.topic: Community Building and Social Media Strategy
learning_objectives: Develop a community building strategy across Discord, Reddit, Twitter/X, and TikTok
Create a content calendar for consistent social media presence
Understand community management best practices and moderation
readings: Building and Sustaining Game Communities - GDC 2023 Talk

lecture_notes.introduction: A game's community is its most valuable long-term marketing asset. Engaged communities generate organic word-of-mouth, provide feedback during development, and sustain a game long after launch.
key_concepts: Platform selection: matching your audience to the right platforms
Discord server architecture: channels, roles, bots, and engagement loops
Content types by platform: Twitter/X for announcements and gifs, TikTok for devlogs, Reddit for deep dives
Content calendar creation: cadence, themes, and balancing development with marketing
Community moderation: setting rules, handling toxicity, empowering moderators
lecture_notes.demonstration: Setting up a Discord server for a game project with proper channel structure, roles, and welcome flow
setup_requirements: Discord account with server creation privileges
Canva or equivalent for social media graphics

review.value: Discord for core community, Twitter/X for broad reach, TikTok for viral potential, Reddit for niche audiences
Minimum 3 posts per week during active marketing, daily during launch window, weekly during quiet development

learning_objectives: Plan and storyboard an effective game trailer
Understand pacing, music selection, and editing techniques for game trailers
Analyze trailer structures used by successful indie and AAA titles
readings: Kert Gartner: How to Make a Game Trailer
Derek Lieu: Game Trailer Editing Masterclass Notes

lecture_notes.introduction: Your trailer is the first thing most people will see of your game. A great trailer can make an unknown indie game go viral; a bad trailer can sink a technically excellent title.
key_concepts: Trailer types: announce, gameplay, launch, accolades, story
The 3-second rule: your trailer must hook viewers within the first 3 seconds
Pacing structures: escalation build, mystery reveal, gameplay montage, narrative arc
Music and audio: licensing, sync rights, and the role of audio in emotional impact
Platform-specific requirements: Steam capsule autoplay, YouTube thumbnail, social media aspect ratios
lecture_notes.demonstration: Frame-by-frame breakdown of three award-winning indie game trailers, analyzing editing choices
setup_requirements: Video editing software (DaVinci Resolve, Premiere Pro, or CapCut)
Access to royalty-free music libraries (Epidemic Sound, Artlist)

review.value: Hook (0-3s) -> Establish mood (3-15s) -> Show core gameplay (15-45s) -> Escalate (45-75s) -> Logo and call to action (75-90s)
Too long (over 90s for reveal), leading with logos, showing menus, poor audio mix, no gameplay footage

learning_objectives: Identify and prioritize influencers and content creators for outreach
Write effective outreach emails that get responses
Manage key distribution and embargo strategies
readings: GDC Talk: Working With Content Creators - What Developers Need to Know

lecture_notes.introduction: Content creators on YouTube and Twitch drive a massive portion of game discovery. Learning to work with them effectively and respectfully is a critical marketing skill.
key_concepts: Identifying the right creators: audience size vs. audience fit
Key distribution platforms: Keymailer, Woovit, Terminals.io, manual outreach
Writing outreach emails: personalization, providing value, making coverage easy
Embargo strategy: coordinating coverage timing for maximum impact
Building long-term relationships vs. transactional outreach
lecture_notes.demonstration: Live walkthrough of setting up a Keymailer campaign and drafting personalized outreach emails
setup_requirements: Spreadsheet for tracking outreach and responses

review.value: Micro-influencers (1K-50K) convert better than mega-influencers; focus on genre-relevant creators over raw subscriber count
Send keys 1-2 weeks before launch, include press kit link, provide embargo date, follow up once after 5 days

learning_objectives: Create a comprehensive launch day checklist and timeline
Plan post-launch content updates and community engagement
Analyze launch metrics and adjust marketing strategy in real time
readings: Steam Launch Best Practices - Steamworks Documentation
How Games Succeed on Steam: 2023 Year in Review - Chris Zukowski

lecture_notes.introduction: Launch day is not the finish line; it is the starting line. A well-executed launch day followed by strong post-launch support separates sustainable successes from flash-in-the-pan releases.
key_concepts: Launch day checklist: store page live, press keys sent, community posts scheduled, patch pipeline ready
Launch timing: day of week, time of day, avoiding major competing releases
First-week metrics that matter: units sold, review ratio, refund rate, concurrent players
Post-launch roadmap communication: setting and managing player expectations
Discount strategy: launch discount debate, first sale timing, seasonal sale participation
lecture_notes.demonstration: Building a complete launch timeline from T-minus 30 days to T-plus 90 days with milestones and contingencies
setup_requirements: Project management tool (Notion, Trello, or similar)
Analytics dashboard setup (Google Analytics, Steam Analytics)

review.value: Tuesday through Thursday releases perform best; avoid major AAA launch windows and Steam sale periods
Hotfix within 48 hours, first content update within 30 days, major update within 90 days to trigger Steam algorithm boost

questions.question: A Steam wishlist-to-purchase conversion rate is typically around 15-20%. If your game has 10,000 wishlists at launch, approximately how many first-week sales can you expect? Explain what factors could cause the actual number to deviate significantly from this estimate.
questions.answer: Approximately 1,500-2,000 first-week sales. Factors that increase conversion: strong reviews (90%+ positive), major streamer coverage, a launch discount, strong trailer and store page. Factors that decrease conversion: poor reviews, buggy launch, stronger competing releases in the same week, wishlists accumulated over a very long period (older wishlists convert at lower rates as interest decays).

rubric.key: Correct calculation from the conversion rate
Identifies at least two factors that increase conversion
Identifies at least two factors that decrease conversion
Mentions wishlist age decay as a factor

questions.question: You are building a Discord server for your upcoming roguelike game. Describe the channel structure you would create, including at least 5 specific channels with their purposes, and explain your moderation strategy.
questions.answer: Channel structure should include: #announcements (developer-only posting for official updates), #general-chat (open discussion with active moderation), #screenshots-and-clips (player-shared content to drive engagement), #bug-reports (structured feedback with template), #suggestions (community feature requests with voting), #off-topic (social bonding outside the game). Moderation strategy should include clear rules posted in a #rules channel, automod for slurs and spam, volunteer moderators from engaged community members with escalation procedures, and a welcoming onboarding flow for new members.

rubric.key: At least 5 well-defined channels with clear purposes
Moderation strategy includes both automated and human elements
Includes an onboarding or welcome flow
Channels demonstrate understanding of community engagement loops

questions.question: Write a complete outreach email to a mid-sized YouTube content creator (50K subscribers) who primarily covers indie strategy games. Your game is a turn-based tactics game with a unique time-rewind mechanic. Include all essential elements of an effective outreach email.
questions.answer: The email should include: a personalized opening referencing a specific video by the creator, a concise game description with the unique hook (time-rewind mechanic), a comparison to games the creator has covered (e.g., 'fans of Into the Breach and XCOM'), a clear offer (free key, exclusive early access, or embargo opportunity), direct links to the press kit and trailer, and a professional sign-off with contact information. The tone should be respectful of the creator's time and not overly salesy.

rubric.key: Personalized opening referencing specific creator content
Clear and compelling game description with unique hook
Includes comparison hooks and press kit link
Professional tone and clear call to action

questions.question: Your indie game launches in 30 days. Create a launch timeline with at least 6 specific milestones, explaining what marketing action happens at each point and why that timing matters.
questions.answer: T-30 days: Send keys to top-priority streamers and press with embargo date set to T-3. T-21 days: Launch Steam Coming Soon page update with final trailer and updated screenshots. T-14 days: Begin daily social media countdown content; activate community ambassadors. T-7 days: Embargo lifts for previews (not reviews); coordinate with creators for preview coverage. T-3 days: Embargo lifts for reviews; monitor early review sentiment. T-0 (Launch day): Store page goes live, launch announcement across all channels, community team active for 16+ hours monitoring feedback and bug reports. T+1 day: First hotfix if critical bugs found, thank-you post to community. T+7 days: First week metrics review, adjust marketing spend based on conversion data.

rubric.key: At least 6 distinct milestones with specific timing
Each milestone has a clear marketing action
Includes post-launch actions, not just pre-launch

major_assignments.title: Complete Game Marketing Campaign
major_assignments.description: Develop a comprehensive marketing campaign for a game project (your own or a provided sample project). This campaign must cover all phases from pre-announcement through 90 days post-launch, including all marketing assets, community strategy, and launch plan.
objectives: Apply marketing principles to a real game project
Create professional-quality marketing assets from scratch
Demonstrate strategic thinking about audience, timing, and messaging
Build a realistic and actionable launch plan with contingencies
deliverables: Complete press kit with factsheet, descriptions, screenshots, logos, and key art
30-day content calendar for two social media platforms
Influencer outreach list (25 creators) with personalized email templates
Launch timeline document from T-60 to T+90 with milestones and contingencies
Post-mortem analysis template prepared for tracking launch metrics

criteria: All essential components present and professional quality
Screenshots demonstrate effective composition and capture
Game description is compelling and follows proven formulas
Key art is visually striking and readable at thumbnail size
Hooks viewer within first 3 seconds
Effective pacing and music selection
Shows actual gameplay prominently
Professional editing with clean transitions and clear call to action
Target audience is clearly defined with supporting rationale
Content calendar is realistic and shows varied content types
Influencer list demonstrates research and strategic prioritization
Launch timeline accounts for contingencies and has clear decision points
All deliverables are polished and presentation-ready
Writing is clear, concise, and free of errors
Visual assets meet platform-specific requirements
Campaign demonstrates cohesive branding and messaging
Comprehensive campaign covering all required phases
Professional-quality marketing assets
Strategic depth demonstrating market understanding
Polished presentation ready for real-world use

weekly_quizzes.description: Regular assessment of marketing concepts and strategic thinking

community_participation.description: Active engagement in class Discord server and peer marketing reviews

marketing_assets.description: Individual press kit, trailer, and social media content creation assignments

major_campaign_project.description: Comprehensive marketing campaign demonstrating mastery of all course concepts

final_presentation.description: Live pitch presentation of marketing campaign to a panel of industry professionals

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for all class sessions and guest speaker presentations. More than 2 unexcused absences will result in a half-letter grade reduction.
course_policies.late_submissions: Marketing is deadline-driven. Late assignments are penalized 15% per day. No submissions accepted more than 3 days late, as marketing assets lose relevance quickly.
course_policies.collaboration: Students may collaborate on community building exercises but all campaign deliverables must be individually produced. Peer review is expected and contributes to participation grade.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All marketing copy, assets, and strategies must be original work. Use of AI tools for content generation must be disclosed. Presenting others' marketing materials as your own constitutes plagiarism.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students must have access to video editing software (DaVinci Resolve is free), image editing tools, and social media accounts. Discord is required for community management exercises.
